title: Echinocactus strobiliformis
taxonomy: Echinocactus strobiliformis is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Cactoideae and further grouped into the tribe Cacteae. The genus Echinocactus is a part of the family Cactaceae, which includes other related species. Echinocactus strobiliformis is native to Mexico and is known for its unique, club-shaped stems. This species is also sometimes referred to as the Stolz's ball cactus.
uses: Echinocactus strobiliformis, commonly known as the pencil cactus, is used in traditional medicine in some regions. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
